notes:
All 48 teams and their Group A-L placements are confirmed via Wikipedia and FIFA.com. The four play-off slots resolved to Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Turkey and Czech Republic (UEFA), plus DR Congo and Iraq (via the FIFA inter-confederation play-off). 26-man World Cup 2026 squads are CONFIRMED via official squad announcements (FIFA.com, ESPN, Olympics.com, FourFourTwo, UEFA, national FAs) for all 48 teams; every star and notable-member entry below is drawn from a cited source.

SHIRT NUMBERS: Confirmed only for a subset and otherwise set to null (not guessed). Full confirmed number lists: Argentina, Mexico, Canada (from Wikipedia squads page). Individually confirmed: Ronaldo 7, Hakimi 2, Salah 10, Mahrez 7, Haaland 9, Son 7, Afif 11, Almoez/others null, Al-Dawsari 10, Edson Alvarez 4, Davies 19, Messi 10, Mbappe 10, Van Dijk 4, Modric 10, Xhaka 10, Ryan (AUS) 1, Scotland GK Angus Gunn 1, James Rodriguez 10, Ospina 1. All other numbers are null because no source confirmed them.

SQUADS NOT FULLY CONFIRMED / CAVEATS (source-based):
- Japan: Kaoru Mitoma (Brighton) omitted injured; star listed is Takefusa Kubo. Captain Wataru Endo was selected but reportedly withdrew injured at the start of the tournament (Itakura took the captaincy) - Endo still listed as a notable member.
- Iran: Sardar Azmoun dropped (reported non-football/political reasons); star is Mehdi Taremi. Several Iran squad members' current clubs were not stated in the sources consulted (set to null).
- Ghana: Mohammed Kudus (Tottenham) ruled out injured; the established star listed is Thomas Partey. One search source erroneously placed Antoine Semenyo at Manchester City - he plays for AFC Bournemouth (corrected here).
- Ivory Coast: Sebastien Haller omitted; star listed is Simon Adingra (reported moving to Monaco from Brighton).
- Jordan: qualifying top scorer Yazan Al-Naimat ruled out (ACL injury); star is Mousa Al-Tamari.
- Uruguay: Luis Suarez retired/omitted for the first time since 2010; star is Federico Valverde.
- Cape Verde, Curacao, Haiti, Iraq, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, DR Congo, Panama, Bosnia, Switzerland, Austria, New Zealand: star and notable players are source-confirmed, but several individual club affiliations were not stated in sources (set to null rather than guessed). Tahith Chong is listed as Curacao's star and reported at Sheffield United (one source conflated him with the Netherlands squad - he is a Curacao international).
- Some club fields reflect 2025-26 transfers reported in sources (e.g., Memphis Depay at Corinthians, Sadio Mane at Al Nassr, Son Heung-min at LAFC, Luis Diaz at Bayern Munich, Scott McTominay at Napoli, Kalidou Koulibaly at Al-Hilal, Granit Xhaka at Sunderland, Edin Dzeko at Schalke). Where a player's exact current club could not be confirmed, it is left null.

'star' = the single best-known/most prominent player per team (current form and/or all-time stature), per the task. A null number simply means the value was not confirmed by a source - no values were invented.
